title: cochlearia officinalis subsp. scotica
intro: Cochlearia officinalis subsp. scotica, also known as Scottish scurvy grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a source of vitamin C, which helps to prevent scurvy and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Scottish scurvy grass include its ability to aid in the treatment of scurvy, as well as its potential to help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and to support the overall health of the digestive system. Scottish scurvy grass can be consumed as a herbal preparation, such as a tea or infusion, where the leaves are steeped in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
